The crew who arrived were loud, throwing things everywhere on site and I had to ask them a number of times to remove their items from my neighbours front lawn.  When I returned home to their work, they'd made a terrible mess dropping the old singles down the side of the house.  There were remains of the job and dirt everywhere. The supervisor came and was not happy with the work either.  He promised to make everything perfect.  They did clean our siding, and it was spotless.  However I spent a good deal of time sweeping up dirt from their work and found a number of nails on the floor and single cuttings.  Far from spotless. No offer to do it themselves or guarantee their work by making a proper repair. During the sales process they said their guarantee was five years. However they quoted me $300 for them to come over and tar the offending spot. That is not a proper repair. And now, the original "leak" is leaking again, making their efforts and ephemeral guarantee totally worthless. If I had not employed them, I would have only one leak instead of two.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Dan Carr","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLIw4ClH6vRbfQPK4P-LaFzB_Zukqn178UML8VMocXBBATNkQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We used Toronto Roofing Industries a few years ago when we needed to replace our sloped roof. Their quote was reasonable and the staff professional and attentive - we had a good experience. Just recently, we had a ceiling leak in a part of the house near a skylight (work done by another contractor) on a weekend. I called them Monday morning, they were there inspecting before noon, and the work was done before the end of the day. They were friendly and helpful and the price was reasonable. Can't do better than that.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Michael Erdman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLgM-5T6ceqD5Q6FrSguO8Oz4zCv9LV5KD7fcB2XRkBn3mIgQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Warning re local roofing company. I woke up a couple of days ago to the sound of roofers going at it. To my surprise, the roofers, Toronto Roofing Industries, were on my neighbour's roof and to my dismay, also tromping through my gardens, with layers of old shingles covering a large portion of the beds along the property line. Neither the neighbour nor the roofers had warned us of this work, or even asked permission to set up their ladders on our property. They just did it. And they destroyed about $1,000 of bulbs and perennials. If I had known the crew was coming, I could have easily protected the flower beds. When I saw the damage and to prevent even more, I demanded that they remove the ladder and materials from my yard. Eventually the owner of Toronto Roofing Industries, Tim Mulroy, convinced me to allow them access in order to finish their work, in return for which he would "make things right" for the damage. Sadly, I agreed, but Mulroy rejected my estimate of the damage, offering me one-third of the value because he thinks "it will all grow back". I'm a gardener and know landscaping costs all too well. And I also know that plants don't just "grow back" if they have been mashed and ground into a pulp by people walking on them.  I have attached a photo of a small portion of beds flattened by the crew.  So I have learned two things: avoid Toronto Roofing Industries since they don't give a tinker's dam about a neighbour's yard. And secondly, don't trust the owner of this business. His promises are worthless.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID2qpntowE&fid=0x0:0x1e25a31b8cd34032"],"When":"2022-5-11"},{"Name":"Paula T. I haven't heard anything since.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"karim manji","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLoHsLDlgkJarR_bYocPwsSnFGPePLcRb0VgxXHIB6566YF2Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Wanted to really support a local Roncy business so selected Roofworks, even though it took a while for them to return my call and their quote was a bit high.

After the roof repair was completed the rep requested another $400+hst to clean the gutters, claimed gutter cleaning was not included in the quote - even though the content of the contract clearly states "Clean eavestrough of debris."

If, knowing this, you're still inclined to support your local Roncy business, Roofworks, ensure you get separate contracts for every single piece of work being requested.  It's not enough that the requested work is specifically in the content of the contract, it needs to be in the header of the contract else your request won't be honoured.  And don't expect them to be upfront about this contract quirk either.","Images":null,"When":""}]

[{"Name":"Shaibal Chowdhury","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWBuEHE7VLVV5RUGYrSct03PbLykKYnlDfMxsmB_O3GXB4VzqSWVw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I’m not usually someone who likes to post reviews, especially about contractors! But when Roofing Monkey asked, I couldn’t say no. I had ice-dam damage last winter, and when Camillo first came, he didn’t try to sell me anything. Instead, he gathered information, took pictures, and went back to his office to discuss with his team the best way to prevent the same issue from happening again. He showed me the options and guided me through the process over several visits. I was extremely pleased with his team’s work—replacing the gutters, installing leaf guards, fascia, soffits, and even a solar fan. They also removed my chimney with care and made the drainage system simple and more effective. That’s how they build trust, and we’ve already booked them again for next year to replace our shingles.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB0lk8jQGgS69evzBZ48DUo&fid=0x0:0x1691e9dd673e5f6a"],"When":"2025-12-1"},{"Name":"Drew Pinfold","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWjoxWr6H4opzEVlVoUPh7On6z2--B99QmItTCX9gOcNfVuydI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Roofing Monkeys did a great job on our house.  We had an aging and degrading shingle roof. Loosing shingles on every windy or stormy day.
Christian stopped by to review the scope of work and was very informative of the situation i had up top. He discussed the various options available and the step they go through, replacing the old roof and the application of the new roofing materials. We went with a full strip and replace, to avoid any irregular overlay issues and couldn't be happier with the end look
They redid all the flashing and drip endging and put down a membrane over the entire roof, before the 20yr shingles were fastened.
They cleaned up all around the property and we're done in less than 2 days!
Really happy with the end product and the house looks great!
Highly recommend this company and crew!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDJCzoNsFE-y3sLb48KJu8t&fid=0x0:0x1691e9dd673e5f6a"],"When":"2025-7-17"},{"Name":"Peter Pulinec-Reali","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXaXlQFWpFaH5VbdMyspLbAPlNU64qLNOVayrc47fRxcuCD5lzH=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I recently worked with Roofing Monkeys, and I truly appreciated their complete transparency throughout the entire process. From the initial inspection to the final walk through, they took the time to explain every step clearly, answer all my questions, and make sure I understood exactly what was being done. That level of honesty and communication made the experience smooth and stress-free.

Thank you to the entire Roofing Monkeys team for your hard work, professionalism, and attention to detail. The quality of their craftsmanship speaks for itself, and the project was completed on time and at a fair price. I would confidently recommend them to anyone looking for reliable, high-quality roofing work backed by integrity and excellent customer service.

p.s. Ask for Christian, he is very knowledgeable and will show you a demo of the entire process as well.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Christopher","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIyRb8kfRYF7pMYF2jrT-Vhk3756-xyaM3ZrrhXFaHPwX8iAwQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Late this summer I hired roofing monkeys to install fascia all around my house and soffits around the porch.

The process and work was amazing and I would hire them again. Camilo came out super quick to provide a quote and the work was completed faster than anticipated in the busy part of the summer. They finished the whole thing in only a couple of days.  I thought they would struggle because of the high angle of the house and because it was an old house using old materials built in the early 1900s. However, they completed everything quickly and didn't complain about any of the irregularities of the house. This has not been my experience with other contractors working and it was a pleasant surprise to have the professionallism of roofing monkeys.

Lastly, the price they provided was fair, even beating out some of the competitors, and the quality of work was great. I am happy I went with them and couldn't recommend them enough.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kym Baker","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI9QCNKOWa80BxMHPBGqeEXSnZBCxwlNqprnq6QKKpDw4SJgw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very happy after my recent experience with Roofing Monkeys. We had a leak, and Carlos was able to diagnose the problem, while also discovering some other potential issues that we weren't aware of. He gave us options on how to proceed which were very reasonable and he was not pushy at all while we made our decision.

I spoke to a few other roofing contractors, and found they were either dishonest or didn't have the proper insurance (keep in mind that roofers need hot work/open flame insurance to do flat roof work, and not all have it!). By comparison, another popular and highly rated roofer in Toronto quoted me $4,000 for the repair claiming it to be a much larger job than it was, while Carlos quoted ~$600.

Carlos and the team also kept lines of communication open and were extremely responsive which I really appreciated.

Highly recommend them for their quality of work, fair pricing, and reliability.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Julie","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLgabd0EUKiEVZCWOTYVnqC9P5oxdEvztX_RJFkZn0_o3Ez5iA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Our 3rd floor deck was in very bad condition and the flat roof underneath was in poor condition. Roofing Monkeys offered both deck removal and a new flat roof in one day.  The demolition of our existing deck and replacement of the flat roof was seamless. Exceptional professional work from start to finish. We highly recommend Roofing Monkeys. Very honest - they operate with integrity.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Steve Polak","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWx0b5AkUOjG8LoTE5yJF1sBL0tPuwSnsqxXoE0A47r5rZd97ku=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I am so impressed with Christian, Brian and the Roofing Monkees team.

I knew my flat roof was due for replacement and had asked a building contractor friend who he recommended. Roofing Monkees so I planned to give them a call when it warmed up.
Our back bedroom began to leak late on a Sunday afternoon in late February so I gave Roofing Monkees a call, not really expecting an answer , Surprise, Christian picked up and we set an appointment for the next day.
Christian arrived on time, inspected the leaking room and determined I most likely had a ventilation/condensation issue rather than a leak.
Christian went up on the roof shovelled the leaking area of snow and cleared the snow off a covered roof vent. (The leak stopped within 30 minutes.)
He advised I could probably get another year out of the roof. I decided to get the roof replaced.
Christian explained in detail what had to be done, strip all old material off the roof, multiple layers of ancient tar and light gravel plus a thirty year old cap. Install new flat roof vents, a layer of 2 inch foam, hardboard then a final cap.
The Roof Monkees 4 man crew arrived early and worked a solid 12 hours with 2 other people assisting on and off. Being late February with rain in the forecast for the next two days they did not leave until  the roof was water tight clean up was completed under flashlights. The next day two people arrived to continue with the roofing and two people arrived to complete eavestroughs, downspouts and other metal flashing. They worked for 4 hours and the rain stopped any further work for the day. A two person crew arrived two days later once it stopped raining and completed all the required work. The final site cleanup was immaculate, back deck, alley way between the houses, front walk and even the front lawn.
Christian arrived the next day to do a final quality inspection and provide photos of the work from start to finish.
I highly recommend Roofing Monkees for any of your roofing needs. Christian was extremely courteous, friendly and professional, providing written estimates at very competitive prices his team members were hard working, friendly and courteous and everyone arrived on time or early. Communication was excellent with both office staff and Christian providing regular updates. Well done Roofing Monkees.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMDQ_Ne9qwE&fid=0x0:0x1691e9dd673e5f6a"],"When":"2025-3-9"},{"Name":"Jen Hendriks","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXkQsKHrEC0OwOT9i6YOIB932UuEYKuaaniE9fBNWoqrJISPlh4=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Carlos and his team were fantastic. Reliable, came in at budget AND had he had a great sense of humor. There was a miscommunication about our porch roof that led to a leak but he arranged the correction and fix before I'd even noticed! 10/10 would recommend","Images":null,"When":""}]

[{"Name":"James Bresolin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK83eRsz_pvg2xAiMapiHhUofpmsZ6kv3ZpYobmAVbVpq7uCw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"After a cold call by Ample roofing (Edward) I decided to let them do the work because his quote was reasonable compared to others I'd gotten and he stated no payment was required until the job was completed. This is not my home, I was acting on behalf of my 90 Y.O mother. He stated he had an opening for the next morning and it was a simple strip and install of shingles for $7000.
The next morning a crew showed up at 8am, all Chinese workers and none spoke English but they got to work right away at stripping the old shingles. At some point Edward showed up and assessed the state of the plywood on the roof, the house is 55 years old. The wood was bad, he called me to the roof to show me and I agreed the wood was rotten. He said this would be extra of course and he would give me a new quote.
Within 10 minutes he called to say the new quote was $26,000. I almost choked, as did my mother but we felt trapped as the roof was almost stripped at this point and I had never got quotes from other companies for a complete roof replacement. Reluctantly he was told to go ahead. This is where the problems started.
He now said he needed some money before the job was completed because of the added expense and I gave him $7000 cash. This was going against everything I felt was right and was trying to avoid with notoriously crooked roofers. He now said the old wood was not up to todays code and he would need to put thicker plywood on the roof. I agreed since the roof was 55 years old and in bad shape. As the repairs were underway I noticed that he wasn't replacing all the wood as I was told and was using the same thickness wood which he earlier said wasn't up to code. When I questioned him on this he said his father used that on an Air B'N'B so it was acceptable, but that's not what I was told earlier. The fact I questioned him seemed to upset him and now he said the $26,000 was plus tax, taking the final total to $29,000 ($19,000 taxable $7000 cash) and he said the job would be completed the same day and needed payment that afternoon. He said to make the draft payable to 17092695  Canada Corp. I figured this was his business number. As I was walking away he changed that to have the draft made out to Mohammad Hasan Rahman whom he said was his father's supplier. Nothing was sitting right with me about any of this and I was feeling my mother was being taken advantage of, in hindsight I should have refused payment but they were still working away and I didn't want the job stopped midway with an open roof. I got the draft and asked him to itemize the taxes on the bill as seniors can claim some home improvements on their taxes. He refused to do that, instead he just put the total paid on the bill. (This was reported to CRA, they will be investigating).
The work was not completed the same day, they returned in the morning and finished by 4pm, that was a Friday. The next afternoon two Chinese men showed up at the door looking for Edward stating they were the sub contractors who did the job and Edward never paid them and was now ghosting them (I found out the business number listed above was their company you can google the number, Owner name is Yuyang Song Business #789283835RC00001)  They asked me to pay them and when I refused they showed me text messages with pictures of the roof that corroborated the fact they only charged Ample Roofing $12,000 for the roof. They were both shocked when I told them what my mother was charged, $29,000 total $17,000 more for doing nothing but subcontracting the job out. I was furious.
I called Edward the following Tuesday he didn't argue that he jacked the price up and we agreed that he would return $5000 to my mother (not enough but I would take anything at that point) and give her an itemized bill and the warranty paperwork. There were two more conversations with Edward assuring me it would get settled and now I've been ghosted by him and still have no warranty paperwork or itemized bill. I mentioned he would be getting a bad review and he stated he would be able to counter any bad review I posted.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhC_PrskLWVn5DF7jWq9Fn1F&fid=0x0:0x7f7b57a18acc341"],"When":"2025-9-17"},{"Name":"Dereck Dewson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ0NSmXjWiCO0AvA_ZD3NrTxkB47DDvzMAU0AHYpSicIXiJMQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"This is the first time I have ever reviewed a company.  Like James I got a quote from Ample Roofing.  Paul came July 31st and gave me a quote of $4,500.  I agreed to the quote and he said they would start the next day, August 1st.  When they showed up to do the roof they took me up on the roof and showed me that my roof needed new plywood.   The price soared to $35,000.  I was floored, and I should have stopped the work right there.  But Paul seemed like an honest guy, so I let them continue.  I know my roof was bad, but the price seemed outrageous.  I had asked Paul about  my eavestrough and back down spout.  He told me his crew would take care of securing the eavestrough, and fixing the down spout at no extra cost.  They finished the roof that day, and said they would be back the next day to do the down spout.  This is where there really got me upset.  They never showed up the next day.  I called Paul, and he said he would take care of it.  Guess what,  he never showed up again.  After numerous text messages, and phone calls they still never showed up.  Paul even promised me that I was a priority to get the work done.  I am such a priority that now when I call the two phone numbers that I have for them, the lines just hang up.  If I can save one person from making the same mistake of trusting Paul, then I have done what I wanted to.  It has been 53 days since my roof was done, and they still haven't finished everything that they had promised me.  These guys do not stand by their word.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Mina Wassef","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI4s46YzbEnig3RzjwBGSOOVmLLOOLIFDR8p3OLJhrrYffRvw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Outstanding roofing service! The crew was professional, and completed my roof replacement quickly with top-quality materials. They paid close attention to detail, and left the property spotless. My new roof looks great. Highly recommend  for any roofing needs","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"David R. They handled everything from general handyman work and painting to removing scratches from my windows, stainless steel sink and even the bathtub. Super professional, friendly, and detail-focused. Highly recommend them for anything from handyman services to glass scratch, stainless steel scratch, and tub scratch repair.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jesse Regan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXWVqs6-IX_Bn-7Gq_sbi-oVOdBhxYLOlu_Fyzmp6S9T2BonsaF=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"OVERPRICED, MISLEADING and huge language barriers (workers barely speak English). I was originally quoted an estimate of between $1000-$1500 by the project manager (minor sewage back-up in unfinished area of basement). The final price tag was over $2500!!! The biggest hidden expense is that they charge the customer for travel time to the job site. It took them almost two hours to arrive at my home even though their website claims they'll be there in 45 minutes or less!!! I was charged more for them to get to my home than I was for the time they spent IN my home working.

Their crew was in my house for less than 2 hours, but I was charged 4 hours worth of labour at over $80/hr per worker, so approximately $160/hr for both employees. At no time did the project manager state that travel time would be included in labour costs. Instead of a fair cost of approximately $350 for labour, I was charged over $700 for less than two hours of work.

And before you think that the fee included equipment costs, usage, rental, etc. Nope!!! The cost for them to leave their fans and other equipment for less than 48 hours totaled $726.17!!! I was also charged a separate $375 for the emergency call-out (it was a Sunday morning, so I knew I'd get heavily charged for the call-out).

Here's the breakdown:

Labour: $724.60 (for 90 minutes worth of work)
Equipment: $726.17 (less than 48hrs)
Cleaning Agent: $64.98
Plumber: $621.50
Emergency Call-out (after hours): $375.82

TOTAL: $2513.07

The work was well-done and the workers were polite and professional, but beware of the costs. I was mislead and paid double, almost triple what I was originally quoted . BUYER BEWARE.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Frank Zhang","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJDwe_ZZ20KkiHMQiBtFuadIehvTLc59AcnUSo4Dl-jOl-aKg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The experience with this company started well but turned to the worst in the end. The job was for mold remediation. They did a good job to remove the mold, I give them that. However, as part of the remediation job, they had to remove a large piece of dry wall. The removal was included in the scope of work. It's my own stupidity to interpret that as the same dry wall will be replaced to put the place back to its original condition. The remediation person also said that the dry wall will be removed and replaced afterwards. It's also my own stupidity to interpret both removing and replacing are part of the same job. After the remediation was completed, they also told me verbally that "don't worry, the wall will be fixed".

But no, the replacement is not part of the quote and they conveniently left that out. When I called to discuss, they (Kevin, Phil and Juan) said it's my own fault for not reading the scope carefully and for misinterpreting that replacing the dry wall was included in the scope. I'll eat this myself and lesson learned. And I just want to share my story as to this is how GTA Restoration conduct businesses with their customers. Now I will not work again with this company nor will I recommend to anyone because I feel cheated.

Updated as of May 6, 2025
Since my initial bad review, the company has reached out trying to understand the situation and reach to an agreement to make me happy. I applaud for the nice gesture. In the end, the company provided monetary compensation to cover the cost of the wall repair. I have updated my review because of this. The lesson learned here is read your contract and ask the company to walk through the contract and the work authorization form before you sign off. You never know what the fine print could affect you in the end.","Images":null,"When":""}]

From the photographs supplied (before and after pictures attached) the job appears to have been done competently – I’m happy.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhC5Xk3Wghz6erK252S7g5fo&fid=0x0:0x2ad5d0033673e7c0"],"When":"2025-12-8"},{"Name":"Scott Gray","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJsBsmALVcte3VoiFYbMXYelEb3XLPOEnkmyullptrEmWE8_Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Poor assessment, customer service, and overall accountability.

We had a terrible experience with Avenue Road Roofing.  I’d like to think that if everything goes to plan, they’re a fine company to work with, but from our experience if anything goes wrong, they’re an absolute nightmare to deal with.  They do not stand behind their work and will deny accountability at all costs.

There were errors made with the initial assessment which led to a leak not being fixed properly, and their recommendations also created an entirely new problem.  While fixing the main leak, they thought it was a good idea to do some preventative touchups around a skylight, and while working on it, they broke the seal.  It leaked for the very first time the next day, and they had the audacity to suggest that it had nothing to do with them.  So we hired them to fix one leak, and they left us with two!  We subsequently had to hire another company to replace the skylight for $2,000.

I was astonished at how poorly Avenue Road Roofing managed the situation. I thought they would apologize and come fix it, but instead, they denied accountability for both issues.  They said “there’s no warranty on repair work” for the main leak, which reappeared only 3 months later in the exact same place.  They claimed that they did fix the original leak and that it was just a bad winter, but if the initial assessment was done correctly, they would have told us that it’s also necessary to replace the damper vent, not just the shingles.  Instead, they explicitly told us beforehand that the leak was not related to the damper vent, so we didn’t replace it, but after the leak reappeared, they said it was related to the damper vent.  So due to their misguided assessment, the leak was not fixed properly, and they refused to accept any responsibility for this.  There are lots of other roofing companies out there, I’d strongly advise against going with Avenue Road Roofing.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Tim Smith","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIneGMyVUC5eXj3efk1K-FHq1rljarfnUJS3FeqEkM9ZYkiow=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Just had our roof and 3 skylights done by Avenue Road Roofing. Tyler did the assessment, brought in a drone to get all angles which was pretty neat! The 4 man team they sent were incredible, they worked in such hot weather but kept the site very clean and protected my wife's flower beds from shingles being tossed. Very happy with the finished product! I will definitely refer this company to my friend!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kevin Alison t","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL2dw3uNXiAd7G_ey0rbPWvfdu4zwJMhKEqfOcxcJ1SAPxSgg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I hired Luso Roofing after struggling with a persistent, tricky leak around my chimney for over a year, which two previous contractors had failed to fix permanently.

From the initial phone call, their team was professional and highly organized. They scheduled an inspection quickly, and their technician Nuno arrived precisely on time. Unlike previous companies that just looked for wet spots inside, he performed a thorough roof inspection, including attic inspection to pinpoint the exact entry point. He clearly explained that the issue was not just a cracked shingle, but a failure in the back pan flashing itself.

The quote was reasonable and clearly itemized. Once approved, the repair crew showed up promptly. They were incredibly efficient, respectful of my property, and took great care to lay down tarps. The actual repair involved removing the old, faulty flashing, installing new customized metal flashing, and resealing all points of entry. They even reinforced a nearby vent stack as a preventative measure.

This is an update from a five star review of this company after our roof was re:shingled 7 years ago.

Homestars hasn't returned our calls and will not publish my updated review (I tried twice). They appear to have blocked me on their site as well.  Shame on them.

We contacted Three Bro Squirrels Roofing in May 2018 through Homestars, regarding re-shingling the roof of our home.

The work was completed speedily and efficiently with a 12 year guarantee, and I was pleased to write a five star review on Homestars.

We also had the gutters replaced shortly after, on Toni's recommendation (we used another contractor for the gutters.)

I noticed problems with the gutters this past summer, and contacted another Homestars contractor who did an inspection on October 24th 2024.  Upon examination, it was discovered that the shingles had come apart and the wood beneath was rotting due to water seepage.  There were holes in the roof which was shaking and about to collapse.  It would not have survived the next snowfall.

With the weather about to change, the first priority was to save our home. The guys worked 24/7 for a couple of days to strip the rotting shingles and wood, and replace the entire roof.

I have saved samples of the rotting shingles, nails etc.  I also have photographs and video footage of the poor workmanship, the holes, rot and overall damage to our roof.

It took me  couple of days of searching to locate Three Bros invoice.  They were contacted via email and twice by phone.  They were completely indifferent and refused to entertain any discussion.  Insurance will not cover the repairs as the damage was caused by a contractor's faulty workmanship. The loss we sustained on our home due to the carelessness of this contractor was so great that we are putting the house up for sale and moving.
